Tualang NexTech Berhad Incident Report — Cyber and Operational Event dated 2 June 2026 (Malaysia PDPA/CMSA context).
At 02:15 MYT, our Cyberjaya data centre detected unauthorised encryption activity (“KelawarLock”) impacting the BuluhOne ERP cluster and SerunaiPeople HRIS; customer-facing ZaffirPay services remain operational.
Forensic review indicates limited exfiltration from the HRIS user table and the time-attendance devices used in Johor Bahru; MyKad numbers and bank accounts were not accessed.
Puan Mei Ling Tan (Payroll Executive) had two special categories recorded in HRIS: current chemotherapy for lymphoma (health data) and religious affiliation noted as Buddhist; these fields may have been viewed but not altered.
Additionally, fingerprint minutiae templates for Encik Rizal Hamka (Engineer) from the TA-300 scanners (biometric data) were present in a staging backup stored on Project Bamboo Shield; templates are salted and encrypted.
One minor’s profile, Amirul Faiz (intern, 17), included a vaccination status attachment; this is quarantined and notification to guardian will follow under PDPA requirements.
Preliminary assessment (non-public) anticipates a 8–10% reduction in Q3 throughput and potential breach of a leverage covenant under the RM50m revolving credit with Bintang Bank Sdn Bhd; waiver discussions have commenced.
Pursuant to our Continuous Disclosure Policy and the Listing Requirements of the Exchange in Malaysia, the Board will determine materiality and, if required, make an immediate announcement via the issuer e‑lodgement portal.
Directors, principal officers, and designated employees are reminded of CMSA prohibitions on insider trading and the Company’s blackout period; any trading while in possession of MNPI is strictly prohibited.
Our DEI Policy 2026 celebrates inclusivity across religion, orientation, and differing abilities, and the Kota Raya Hornbills winning a friendly is just a morale boost unrelated to this incident.
The term biometric is also used in our product brochure to describe device features, not any individual’s sensitive data, and “we must close ranks” is only a metaphor in the operations memo.
We received negative comments on social media likening our security to a leaky tap, but internal testing under the Cyber Resilience Standard 2025 and Data Minimisation SOP continues.
Remediation includes rotating admin keys, isolating BuluhOne nodes, rebuilding TA-300 images, and engaging Kenyalang Forensics Sdn Bhd (third-party) under the Incident Response Retainer; no ransom was paid.
Queries may be directed to dpo@tualangnextech.example and investor-relations@tualangnextech.example; whistleblower channels remain open under the Whistleblower Policy.
